title: Fix missing tabs in deployed ConversationInput component

requirements.text: Debug and fix the tabbed resource interface in ConversationInput component to ensure all 7 tabs (Music, Dance, Stories, Self Care, Meditation, What to Watch, Journaling) are properly rendered and visible in the deployed application
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 tabs (Music, Dance, Stories, Self Care, Meditation, What to Watch, Journaling) are visible in the ConversationInput component
Tab navigation works correctly allowing users to switch between different resource categories
Each tab displays its corresponding content component (MusicTabContent, DanceTabContent, etc.)
Tabs remain visible and functional after mood selection in the 'How are you feeling?' dropdown
Tab styling and layout match the intended design with proper spacing and visual hierarchy

requirements.text: Verify that the ConversationInput component's tab container and tab buttons have correct CSS classes and are not being hidden by display:none, visibility:hidden, or z-index issues
acceptanceCriteria: Tab container element is rendered in the DOM with visible display properties
Tab button elements are present and clickable in the UI
No CSS rules are inadvertently hiding the tab interface
Z-index stacking context does not place tabs behind other elements

requirements.text: Add browser console logging to ConversationInput component to track tab state, rendering lifecycle, and any errors that might prevent tabs from displaying
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs confirm tab component is mounting and rendering
Active tab state changes are logged when users interact with tabs
Any errors or warnings related to tab rendering are captured and logged
Logs help identify whether the issue is conditional rendering, state management, or CSS-related

constraints: Do not modify the 'How are you feeling?' mood dropdown - it must remain exactly as currently implemented
Preserve all existing tab content components (MusicTabContent, DanceTabContent, StoriesTabContent, SelfCareTabContent, MeditationTabContent, WhatToWatchTabContent, JournalingTabContent)
Maintain the current mood-based resource link mappings in moodResourceLinks.ts
nonGoals: Adding new tabs beyond the existing 7
Changing tab content or resource links
Modifying the overall ConversationInput component layout or styling beyond fixing tab visibility
